page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-1 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 1 dedicated vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture and is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Because the CPU allocation is dedicated, the instance provides predictable single-core compute performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable configurations. However, with only 1 vCPU and no local NVMe storage, the instance is constrained in parallel processing capabilities and high-throughput local I/O operations. Network and storage traffic rely entirely on attached network-based block storage.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4a-highmem-1 offers a high memory density relative to its single-core compute allocation, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require multi-threaded processing. Suitable workloads include lightweight databases, caching layers, directory services, and simple background workers that benefit from a larger memory footprint than standard general-purpose instances of a similar size.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-1 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the c4a server family, designed with a memory-optimized profile. It features 1 dedicated vCPU and 8.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local SSD storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage for its disk operations. With a dedicated CPU allocation, it avoids the performance fluctuations associated with shared-core environments, though its single-core configuration limits parallel processing capabilities. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-sensitive, low-compute tasks. It is best suited for lightweight databases, caching nodes, and small-scale application servers that require a larger memory footprint without the overhead of additional compute cores.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-1 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 1 dedicated vCPU and 8.0 GB of RAM. It offers a memory-optimized configuration for lightweight, memory-sensitive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-1 is an x86_64 instance with 1 dedicated vCPU and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-sensitive workloads.
tagline: A single-core, memory-optimized virtual machine designed for lightweight workloads requiring a high memory-to-CPU ratio without local storage.
bullet_points: 8.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Dedicated CPU allocation for predictable single-core performance
Well-suited for lightweight databases, caching, and memory-sensitive background tasks
